fields.name: access_token
fields.title: Access Token
fields.description: Your Jira Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token

fields.name: refresh_token
fields.title: Refresh Token
fields.description: Your Jira Cloud OAuth 2.0 refresh token (requires offline_access scope)

fields.name: client_id
fields.title: Client ID
fields.description: Your Jira OAuth App Client ID from the Atlassian Developer Console

fields.name: client_secret
fields.title: Client Secret
fields.description: Your Jira OAuth App Client Secret from the Atlassian Developer Console

options.title: Jira API Token Authentication
options.description: Authenticate using your Atlassian account email and API token

fields.name: username
fields.title: Email Address
fields.description: Your Atlassian account email address

fields.name: password
fields.title: API Token
fields.description: Your Jira API token from https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens

supported_questions:
- Show me all open issues in my Jira instance
- List recent issues created in the last 7 days
- List all projects in my Jira instance
- Show me details for the most recently updated issue
- List all users in my Jira instance
- Show me comments on the most recent issue
- Show me worklogs from the last 7 days
- Assign a recent issue to a teammate
- Unassign a recent issue
- Create a new task called 'Sample task' in a project
- Create a bug with high priority
- Update the summary of a recent issue to 'Updated summary'
- Change the priority of a recent issue to high
- Add a comment to a recent issue saying 'Please investigate'
- Update my most recent comment
- Delete a test issue
- Remove my most recent comment
- Transition {issue_key} to In Progress
- Move {issue_key} to Done
- What transitions are available for {issue_key}?
- Log 2 hours of work on {issue_key}
- Log 30 minutes on {issue_key} with a comment about what I did
- Link {issue_key_1} as blocking {issue_key_2}
- Create a 'relates to' link between {issue_key_1} and {issue_key_2}
- What issues are assigned to {team_member} this week?
- Find all high priority bugs in our current sprint
- Show me overdue issues across all projects
- What projects have the most issues?
- Search for users named {user_name}
unsupported_questions:
- Attach a file to {issue_key}
- Add a watcher to {issue_key}

parameters.name: jql
parameters.description: JQL query string to filter issues

parameters.name: nextPageToken
parameters.description: The token for a page to fetch that is not the first page. The first page has a nextPageToken of null. Use the `nextPageToken` to fetch the next page of issues. The `nextPageToken` field is not included in the response for the last page, indicating there is no next page.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page. To manage page size, API may return fewer items per page where a large number of fields or properties are requested. The greatest number of items returned per page is achieved when requesting `id` or `key` only. It returns max 5000 issues.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of fields to return for each issue. By default, all navigable fields are returned. To get a list of all fields, use the Get fields operation.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of parameters to expand. This parameter accepts multiple values, including `renderedFields`, `names`, `schema`, `transitions`, `operations`, `editmeta`, `changelog`, and `versionedRepresentations`.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of issue property keys. To get a list of all issue property keys, use the Get issue operation. A maximum of 5 properties can be requested.

parameters.name: fieldsByKeys
parameters.description: Whether the fields parameter contains field keys (true) or field IDs (false). Default is false.

parameters.name: failFast
parameters.description: Fail the request early if all field data cannot be retrieved. Default is false.

first_operation.description:
Retrieve issues based on JQL query with pagination support.

IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a bounded JQL query. A bounded query must include a search restriction that limits the scope of the search. Examples of valid restrictions include: project (e.g., "project = MYPROJECT"), assignee (e.g., "assignee = currentUser()"), reporter, issue key, sprint, or date-based filters combined with a project restriction. An unbounded query like "order by key desc" will be rejected with a 400 error. Example bounded query: "project = MYPROJECT AND updated >= -7d ORDER BY created DESC".

schema.description: Token for fetching the next page
Whether this is the last page
Total number of issues matching query

issue_worklogs.description:
Adds a worklog entry to an issue to track time spent.
Use timeSpentSeconds or timeSpent (e.g., "3h 30m") to specify time.
Optionally include a started datetime and a comment describing the work done.

issue_transitions.description: Returns the available transitions for an issue. Transitions define the workflow steps an issue can move through (e.g., To Do -> In Progress -> Done). Use this to discover valid transition IDs before performing a transition.

parameters.name: transition

schema.description: The transition to perform

id.description: The ID of the transition to perform. Get available transition IDs from the GET transitions endpoint.

parameters.description: The transition to perform

parameters.name: fields

schema.description: Fields to set during the transition (if required by the transition screen)

parameters.description: Fields to set during the transition (if required by the transition screen)

parameters.name: update

schema.description: Additional update operations to perform during the transition

parameters.description: Additional update operations to perform during the transition

parameters.name: historyMetadata

schema.description: Metadata about the transition for the issue history

parameters.description: Metadata about the transition for the issue history

parameters.name: issueIdOrKey
parameters.description: The issue ID or key (e.g., "PROJ-123" or "10000")

issue_transitions.description:
Performs a status transition on an issue (e.g., To Do -> In Progress -> Done).
This is the primary way to change an issue's workflow status in Jira.

To use this endpoint:
1. First, GET the available transitions for the issue to find valid transition IDs
2. Then POST with the desired transition ID

You can optionally include field updates and comments as part of the transition.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: The type of link (e.g., Blocks, Duplicate, Relates)

name.description: The name of the link type (e.g., Blocks, Duplicate, Relates, Cloners)

id.description: The ID of the link type

inward.description: The inward description (e.g., is blocked by)

parameters.description: The type of link (e.g., Blocks, Duplicate, Relates)

parameters.name: inwardIssue

schema.description: The inward issue (the issue that is affected by the link)

parameters.description: The inward issue (the issue that is affected by the link)

parameters.name: outwardIssue

schema.description: The outward issue (the issue that causes the link)

parameters.description: The outward issue (the issue that causes the link)

parameters.name: comment

schema.description: A comment about the link in Atlassian Document Format (ADF)

parameters.description: A comment about the link in Atlassian Document Format (ADF)

issue_links.description:
Creates a link between two issues. Issue links define relationships such as
"blocks", "is blocked by", "relates to", "duplicates", "is duplicated by", "clones", "is cloned by".

Common link type names: Blocks, Cloners, Duplicate, Relates.
Each type has an inward and outward description (e.g., "blocks" / "is blocked by").

fields.description: The name of the field

fields.name: navigable
fields.description: Whether the field can be used as a column on the issue navigator

fields.name: orderable
fields.description: Whether the content of the field can be used to order lists

fields.name: schema
fields.description: The data schema for the field

fields.name: scope
fields.description: The scope of the field

fields.name: searchable
fields.description: Whether the content of the field can be searched

fields.name: untranslatedName
fields.description: The untranslated name of the field

fields.name: author
fields.description: Details of the user who created the worklog

fields.name: comment
fields.description: A comment about the worklog in Atlassian Document Format

fields.name: created
fields.description: The datetime on which the worklog was created

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the worklog record

fields.name: issueId
fields.description: The ID of the issue this worklog is for

fields.name: properties
fields.description: Details of properties for the worklog

fields.name: self
fields.description: The URL of the worklog item

fields.name: started
fields.description: The datetime on which the worklog effort was started

fields.name: timeSpent
fields.description: The time spent working on the issue as days, hours, or minutes

fields.name: timeSpentSeconds
fields.description: The time in seconds spent working on the issue

fields.name: updateAuthor
fields.description: Details of the user who last updated the worklog

fields.name: updated
fields.description: The datetime on which the worklog was last updated

fields.name: visibility
fields.description: Details about any restrictions in the visibility of the worklog
